description:
**Problem**
The invariant "live ∩ tombstones = ∅" is enforced by careful method design:
```rust
pub struct CanonicalState {
    live: BTreeMap<BeadId, Bead>,
    tombstones: BTreeMap<BeadId, Tombstone>,
    // ...
}
```

This relies on `insert`/`delete` being correct. A bug could violate the invariant.

**Design**
Make the invariant structurally impossible:
```rust
pub enum BeadEntry {
    Live(Bead),
    Tombstone(Tombstone),
}

pub struct CanonicalState {
    beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>,
    deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>,
}
```

Now a bead cannot be both live and tombstoned - the type system prevents it.

**Design Notes**
- Bigger refactor than the other type improvements
- Consider doing this when touching `state.rs` for other reasons
- `get_live`/`get_tombstone` become pattern matches on entry
- Iteration patterns change (`live.values()` → `beads.values().filter_map(...)`)
- Wire format (`src/git/wire.rs`) may need adjustment

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `BeadEntry` enum in `src/core/state.rs`
- [ ] `CanonicalState` uses single `beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>`
- [ ] All accessors (`get_live`, `get_tombstone`, iteration) updated
- [ ] Wire format handles the change
- [ ] Invariant comment updated to note its now structural
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/git/wire.rs
title: Collapse live/tombstones into single BeadEntry map

description: Audit remaining custom lints for false negatives/fragility and fix issues with regression coverage where possible.
title: Follow-up lint hardening sweep in beads_lints
